method_atom: A continuous one-parameter deformation of a symmetry-constrained field theory defined on a space of maps, where tuning the deformation parameter interpolates smoothly between two distinct limiting regimes of the theory; the partition function computed by path integration localizes onto a restricted subclass of admissible configurations selected by an underlying generalized geometric structure, and the allowed boundary/defect objects are constrained by a topological characteristic-class invariant.
object_atom: The N=2 supersymmetric sigma-model whose target is a Calabi-Yau (in particular hyperkahler) manifold, its topologically twisted A- and B-models and their topological D-branes (A-branes and B-branes), and a noncommutative/B-field deformation of the target space described in terms of Hitchin's generalized complex structures, with the brane constraint stated via the Chern character.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the deformation-interpolating localized field theory whose configurations and boundary objects are fixed by a generalized geometric structure and a characteristic-class constraint; the string-theory sigma-model on a Calabi-Yau with A/B topological twists, D-branes, and noncommutative deformation is the domain-specific object.
